subject: Observability & Oversight spine — unified event bus + correlation, workflows as observable objects, live oversight surfaces with freshness contracts, the three-question confidence model; cross-cutting
title: Observability & Oversight Spine (v4)
situation: An S46 audit of the current solution found solid append-only event streams (track.jsonl, provider_usage, lifecycle, advisors/*/runs.jsonl, activity-events-queue, capability-runs) BUT severe oversight gaps: no live dashboard (queue depth, advisor exec state, budget burn, tool latency), no event correlation/causality (events exist but no cause→effect chain), no trend detection (velocity/adoption/quality curves), stale surfaces (KPI ~1wk, ozi-brief ~3-5d, health themes never graded, hub-status ~3d), and no single 'what needs me' attention surface. The user cannot reliably answer 'what is happening, is it on track, what needs me.' v4 must make patterns + workflows well-defined and OBSERVABLE so the user has oversight and confidence.
why: Oversight is the precondition for trusting autonomy. A wheel that rolls on its own is only safe to let roll if the user can see it rolling true and be pulled in exactly when needed. Fragmented, stale, correlation-less telemetry gives data without confidence. This spine unifies emission (one typed event bus), adds causality (correlation across the cause→effect chain), makes every workflow a first-class observable object with live status, and guarantees surface freshness — turning scattered logs into oversight. It is the mirror of the Verification spine: verification proves work is correct; observability proves the user can SEE it is correct, live.
audience: User — the primary consumer: oversight surfaces + the three-question confidence model
Ozi — reads the live state to drive the loops; surfaces attention items
every advisor/pattern/workflow — emits typed events (silent operation is banned)
Historian — mines the correlated event stream for trends
Hub (Surveyor/Cartologist) — aggregates spoke observability into the fleet dashboard

unified_event_bus.principle: Every pattern, workflow, advisor run, test, gate, and state transition emits ONE typed event to a single stream. Silent operation is banned (parallels no-silent-failure). Existing streams (track/gate-log/provider_usage/lifecycle/runs/test_results) become typed views over, or feeders into, this bus.

event_schema.subject_ref: string — lug/flow/test/bolt/advisor id this is about
event_schema.correlation_id: string — groups all events of one causal chain
event_schema.parent_event: event_id|null — the event that caused this one

unified_event_bus.storage: events land in the storage layer (spec-storage-layer-v1) events/gate_log/test_results tables + a JSONL mirror; correlation_id enables the causality queries the audit found missing

durability.principle: Autonomy rests on the bus, so the bus must not silently lose events (blind-review HIGH). Emission is durable + replayable, not fire-and-forget.
rules: write path: append to a local JSONL journal FIRST (synchronous, crash-safe), then index into SQLite; the JSONL journal is the source of truth, SQLite is the query view
write-ack: an actor whose event write fails (disk full, db lock) gets an error, not silent success — failure to emit is itself a logged failure event
replay: on recovery, unindexed journal entries replay into SQLite; the dispatcher replays unacked wakes (spec-scheduler-dispatcher-v1)
backpressure: under load, lower-priority event indexing batches; journal append never blocks (it is the floor)
hub-unreachable: events buffer locally with a sync-pending flag and push on hub recovery (never dropped)

correlation_layer.principle: Events are linked cause→effect so the user can see WHY, not just WHAT. correlation_id groups a chain; parent_event links each step to its cause.
correlation_layer.example_chain: user sets a goal (correlation_id=C) → Expediter scores+queues (parent=goal) → Autopilot dispatches lug (parent=queue) → Pattern Gate certifies (parent=dispatch) → bolt closes (parent=gate) → KPI velocity updates (parent=bolt). One query on C returns the whole story.

decision_events.principle: Events alone trace WHAT happened, not WHY (blind-review HIGH). Every decision that changes flow — a gate halt/escalate, a Trainer rejection/canonicalization, a Hygiene delete, an Autopilot skip, a dispatch breaker trip — MUST emit a type=decision event FIRST, carrying the data/criterion that drove it and the alternatives weighed, linked as the parent of the resulting action event.
decision_events.rule: no rejection/escalation/destructive action without a preceding decision event; 'why did Trainer reject this?' is answerable by querying the decision event on the chain

enables: impact analysis (did this lug completion unblock others?)
blocker drill-down (what is this stalled on?)
explain-why (decision events expose the reasoning, not just the outcome)

workflows_as_observable_objects.principle: Every workflow (warmup sequence, evolution loop, migration, closeout, adoption) is a FIRST-CLASS object with live status — which step, who, when, outcome — not an implicit sequence buried in code.
workflows_as_observable_objects.surface: live workflow status is queryable + shown on the dashboard (e.g. 'warmup: step 2/7 coverage-review running'); a halted/escalated workflow raises an attention item

oversight_surfaces.live_dashboard: rolling 1h/24h/7d: queue depth + predicted clearance ETA, advisors currently running, session budget burn vs plan, tool latency/success (Sentinel contracts measured, not just declared), workflow live status, fleet Christmas-tree (hub). Closes the audit's #1 gap (no live view).
oversight_surfaces.wakeup_brief_sections: Pattern Health, Quality Health (coverage %, null rate, stale tests, failing suites), Certification (green/null rate), Adoption-correctness (Hygiene score), Evolution (open proposals/verdicts), and a delta ('what changed since last session').
oversight_surfaces.attention_surface: the single 'what needs ME' list — escalations, halted workflows, needs-you lugs, stalled-2x lugs, RFC reviews, sign-off requests — each with a reason (why it bubbled up). Closes the audit's 'no attention/escalation system' gap. PRIORITIZED, not a flat backlog (blind-review MED): each item has a priority function (severity x age x blast-radius); a response SLA per item (unanswered past SLA escalates to Octo/Wilbur, never stalls silently); a max-depth alarm (queue over N items is itself a P1 'attention overloaded' signal). Triage is computed for the user, not dumped on them.
oversight_surfaces.trend_detection: velocity, adoption, quality, approval-rate curves over time (not snapshots) — Historian-computed; surfaces 'what is getting worse'.
oversight_surfaces.wheel_kpis: 'faster and straighter' needs defined baselines (review MED). The canonical wheel-health KPIs, each trended + baseline-anchored (Trainer measures post-adoption deltas against these): FASTER = lead_time (lug open→done), time_to_green (session→certified), throughput (lugs/session); STRAIGHTER = first_attempt_approval_rate, retry_rate, halt_rate, null_rate, rework_rate (reopened lugs), escalation_rate. Each KPI has a baseline captured at flow/version boundaries; an improvement claim ('the wheel rolls straighter') is provable as a baseline delta, not a vibe.
oversight_surfaces.control_panel: the four-panel model (Goals/Guardrails/Now/Results) mapped to surfaces — the user's steering wheel.

freshness_contracts.principle: Every oversight surface declares an update cadence (SLA); a surface past its cadence is flagged STALE on its own face (the audit found silently-stale KPI/ozi-brief/themes). Freshness is itself observable.
examples: wakeup sections: regenerated at session start
freshness_contracts.rule: a stale surface shows a STALE badge + age; it is never presented as current.
freshness_contracts.remediation: flagging is not enough (blind-review MED: the audit found silently-stale surfaces; just badging them repeats the problem). A freshness PROBE runs on cadence; a surface overdue past its contract raises an Attention item routed to that surface's owning advisor (Surveyor=dashboard, QA=Quality Health, etc.) to regenerate it. Staleness triggers repair, not just a badge.
freshness_contracts.remediation_backoff: external review A (infinite-loop cascade): if the owning advisor is throttled/budget-depleted/hung, naive regeneration re-fails and re-warns, flooding the bus. Regeneration uses EXPONENTIAL BACKOFF with a hard ceiling (max 3 attempts/hour per surface); after the ceiling the surface is marked STALE-UNRECOVERABLE on the Attention surface (escalation), not retried in a loop.

confidence_model.principle: The user can ALWAYS answer three questions, each backed by a surface:

questions.is_it_on_track: Trajectory — trend curves + KPI projection + certification
questions.what_needs_me: Attention — the escalation/needs-you list with reasons

confidence_model.assertion: if any of the three cannot be answered from a fresh surface, that is itself an observability defect to fix.

constraints: Silent operation is banned: every advisor/pattern/workflow/test/gate emits a typed event — no unobserved work
Every event carries correlation_id + parent_event (where applicable) so causality is reconstructable
Every workflow is a first-class observable object with live step status — no implicit code-buried sequences
Every oversight surface declares + honors a freshness contract; stale surfaces self-flag, never presented as current
Observability data is read-only to consumers; only the emitting actor writes its events
The three confidence questions must always be answerable from a fresh surface
tests: Emission completeness: run a session; assert every advisor run, gate, test, and bolt produced a typed event on the bus (no silent actors)
Correlation: trigger a goal→queue→dispatch→gate→bolt chain; one query on correlation_id returns the full ordered chain with parent links
Workflow object: start the warmup workflow; query live status returns current_step + per-step states; halting step 2 raises an attention item
Live dashboard: queue depth, running advisors, budget burn, and tool latency render from <=5min-fresh data
Attention surface: an escalation + a stalled-2x lug + a sign-off request all appear with reasons
Freshness: a surface past its cadence shows a STALE badge with age; a fresh one does not
Confidence: from a cold session open, all three questions (what's happening / on track / needs me) are answerable from fresh surfaces
Trend: velocity + approval-rate curves render over a 7d window from the event stream

health.test_coverage: partial - oversight-surface layer (dashboard/attention/freshness/confidence model) covered by test_observability_dashboard.py (7 tests, AC1-4 of the impl lug = spec tests Confidence/Live-dashboard/Attention/Freshness). Uncovered: emission-completeness, correlation-chain, workflow-object live status, trend curves (deferred follow-ons, need a populated event bus + Historian).
open_questions: Dashboard delivery: terminal/wakeup-rendered vs Minder web panel vs both (proposed: wakeup + Minder web, same data)
Event bus: derive existing streams into the bus (views) vs migrate emitters to write the bus directly (proposed: emitters write the bus; legacy streams become views during migration)
Attention push: surface-on-pull (wakeup/dashboard) vs Minder/Telegram push for P1 (proposed: pull always + push for escalations)

perceive: Read this spec + spec-storage-layer-v1 (events/test_results tables) + the S46 observability audit in architecture_harness_v4. Inventory existing streams (track, gate-log, provider_usage, lifecycle, runs.jsonl, activity-events) to derive into the bus.
execute: 1. Define the unified event schema + taxonomy; make every advisor/pattern/workflow/test/gate emit it (storage layer events table + JSONL mirror). 2. Add correlation_id + parent_event threading. 3. Implement workflow-as-object with live step status. 4. Build oversight surfaces: live dashboard, wakeup sections, attention surface, trend curves, freshness badges. 5. Wire the three-question confidence model. 6. Add freshness contracts + stale-flagging. 7. Hub aggregation into the fleet dashboard.
verify: Run all tests[]. Confirm no silent actors, correlation chains reconstruct, workflow live status renders, all surfaces honor freshness contracts, and the three confidence questions are answerable from fresh surfaces.
